2606.01632.title: A Framework for Graph-Conditioned Hierarchical Shapley Attribution in Patent Valuation
2606.01632.paper_summary: Estimating the economic contribution of a single patent inside a product that embodies tens of thousands of patents is a long-standing unsolved problem in intellectual property economics. We propose PatentXAI, a framework that treats patent valuation as a problem of explainable AI: given a characteristic function v(S) encoding the revenue achievable by patent subset S, a patent's Shapley value measures its fair share of product profit in a way that satisfies efficiency, symmetry, dummy, and additivity. To make computation tractable we restrict each patent's coalition to its Markov Blanket inside a knowledge graph, grounded in the C-SVE conditional independence theorem (Li et al., 2020). Scaling experiments from n=12 to n=100 patents using Pareto-distributed coverage graphs report median Markov Blanket size of 32.9 percent of n at n=100, with 90th-percentile blanket size of 55.2 percent of n, and runtime of 10 milliseconds per patent. Difference against exact ground truth at n=12 is 0.088; difference against a high-sample Monte Carlo reference at n=100 is 0.062 plus or minus 0.003. A dense-component experiment shows that when 80 percent of patents share one component, the blanket correctly expands to cover that dense cluster, and the difference versus reference falls to 0.039 because the pooled computation becomes more accurate on homogeneous portfolios. Profit allocation proceeds hierarchically: exact Shapley distributes total profit among macro-components, then centrality-weighted Shapley distributes each component budget among covering patents. Estimating v(S) from real data is the primary open problem; we distinguish this from the computational contribution and outline a concrete roadmap for empirical validation using public ETSI, USPTO, and Lens.org datasets.
2606.01632.paper_summary_zh: 估算一項專利在一個包含數萬項專利的產品中的經濟貢獻是知識產權經濟學中一個長期未解的問題。我們提出了PatentXAI，一個將專利評價視為可解釋人工智慧問題的框架：給定一個特徵函數v(S)，編碼專利子集S所能實現的收入，專利的Shapley值以滿足效率、對稱性、虛擬和可加性的方式衡量其在產品利潤中的公平份額。為了使計算可行，我們將每項專利的聯盟限制在其知識圖譜中的馬可夫毯內，這基於C-SVE條件獨立定理（Li et al., 2020）。從n=12到n=100專利的擴展實驗使用了Pareto分佈的覆蓋圖，報告在n=100時，馬可夫毯的中位數大小為n的32.9％，90百分位的毯大小為n的55.2％，每項專利的運行時間為10毫秒。n=12時與精確真實值的差異為0.088；n=100時與高樣本蒙特卡羅參考的差異為0.062，加減0.003。密集組件實驗顯示，當80％的專利共享一個組件時，毯子正確擴展以覆蓋該密集集群，與參考的差異降至0.039，因為在同質投資組合上的合併計算變得更加準確。利潤分配是分層進行的：精確的Shapley在宏觀組件之間分配總利潤，然後中心性加權的Shapley在覆蓋專利之間分配每個組件的預算。從真實數據中估算v(S)是主要的開放問題；我們將此與計算貢獻區分開來，並概述了一個具體的實證驗證路線圖，使用公共的ETSI、USPTO和Lens.org數據集。
2606.01632.author: Joy Bose et.al.
